NKJV.1: A Psalm of the sons of Korah. A Song. His foundation is in the holy mountains.
NKJV.2: The LORD loves the gates of Zion More than all the dwellings of Jacob.
NKJV.3: Glorious things are spoken of you, O city of God! Selah
NKJV.4: “I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon to those who know Me; Behold, O Philistia and Tyre, with Ethiopia: ‘This one was born there.’ ”
NKJV.5: And of Zion it will be said, “This one and that one were born in her; And the Most High Himself shall establish her.”
NKJV.6: The LORD will record, When He registers the peoples: “This one was born there.” Selah
NKJV.7: Both the singers and the players on instruments say, “All my springs are in you.”

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> or Song<S>7892</S> for the sons<S>1121</S> of Korah<S>7141</S>. His foundation<S>3248</S> is in the holy<S>6944</S> mountains<S>2042</S>. <sup>for the sons: or, of the sons</sup>
KJV.2: The LORD<S>3068</S> loveth<S>157</S> the gates<S>8179</S> of Zion<S>6726</S> more than all the dwellings<S>4908</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>.
KJV.3: Glorious things<S>3513</S> are spoken<S>1696</S> of thee, O city<S>5892</S> of God<S>430</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.4: I will make mention<S>2142</S> of Rahab<S>7294</S> and Babylon<S>894</S> to them that know<S>3045</S> me: behold Philistia<S>6429</S>, and Tyre<S>6865</S>, with Ethiopia<S>3568</S>; this man was born<S>3205</S> there.
KJV.5: And of Zion<S>6726</S> it shall be said<S>559</S>, This and that man<S>376</S> was born<S>3205</S> in her: and the highest<S>5945</S> himself shall establish<S>3559</S> her.
KJV.6: The LORD<S>3068</S> shall count<S>5608</S>, when he writeth up<S>3789</S> the people<S>5971</S>, that this man was born<S>3205</S> there. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.7: As well the singers<S>7891</S> as the players<S>2490</S> on instruments shall be there: all my springs<S>4599</S> are in thee.

ESV.1: On the holy mount stands the city he founded;
ESV.2: the LORD loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwelling places of Jacob.
ESV.3: Glorious things of you are spoken, O city of God. Selah
ESV.4: Among those who know me I mention Rahab and Babylon; behold, Philistia and Tyre, with Cush  — “This one was born there,” they say.
ESV.5: And of Zion it shall be said, “This one and that one were born in her”; for the Most High himself will establish her.
ESV.6: The LORD records as he registers the peoples, “This one was born there.” Selah
ESV.7: Singers and dancers alike say, “All my springs are in you.”

NASB.1: A Psalm of the sons of Korah. A Song. His foundation is in the holy mountains.
NASB.2: The LORD loves the gates of Zion More than all the [other] dwelling places of Jacob.
NASB.3: Glorious things are spoken of you, O city of God. Selah.
NASB.4: 'I shall mention Rahab and Babylon among those who know Me; Behold, Philistia and Tyre with Ethiopia: 'This one was born there.''
NASB.5: But of Zion it shall be said, 'This one and that one were born in her'; And the Most High Himself will establish her.
NASB.6: The LORD will count when He registers the peoples, 'This one was born there.' Selah.
NASB.7: Then those who sing as well as those who play the flutes [shall say], 'All my springs [of joy] are in you.'

NLT.1:
On the holy mountain
stands the city founded by the LORD.
NLT.2:
He loves the city of Jerusalem
more than any other city in Israel.
NLT.3:
O city of God,
what glorious things are said of you! Interlude
NLT.4:
I will count Egypt and Babylon among those who know me —
also Philistia and Tyre, and even distant Ethiopia.
They have all become citizens of Jerusalem!
NLT.5:
Regarding Jerusalem it will be said,
“Everyone enjoys the rights of citizenship there.”
And the Most High will personally bless this city.
NLT.6:
When the LORD registers the nations, he will say,
“They have all become citizens of Jerusalem.” Interlude
NLT.7:
The people will play flutes and sing,
“The source of my life springs from Jerusalem!”

WEB.1: A Psalm by the sons of Korah; a Song. His foundation is in the holy mountains.
WEB.2: Yahweh loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
WEB.3: Glorious things are spoken about you, city of God. Selah.
WEB.4: I will record Rahab  and Babylon among those who acknowledge me. Behold, Philistia, Tyre, and also Ethiopia: “This one was born there.”
WEB.5: Yes, of Zion it will be said, “This one and that one was born in her”; the Most High himself will establish her.
WEB.6: Yahweh will count, when he writes up the peoples, “This one was born there.” Selah.
WEB.7: Those who sing as well as those who dance say, “All my springs are in you.”

CSB17.1: A psalm of the sons of Korah. A song. [1] The city he founded [1] is on the holy mountains. ⓝ
CSB17.2:
The LORD loves Zion’s city gates
more than all the dwellings of Jacob. ⓐ
CSB17.3:
Glorious things are said about you,
city of God. ⓑ Selah
CSB17.4:
“I will make a record of those who know me:
Rahab, Babylon, Philistia, Tyre, and Cush —
each one was born there.” ⓒ
CSB17.5:
And it will be said of Zion,
“This one and that one were born in her.”
The Most High himself will establish her. ⓓ
CSB17.6:
When he registers the peoples,
the LORD will record,
“This one was born there.” ⓔ Selah
CSB17.7:
Singers and dancers [2] alike will say, [3]
“My whole source of joy is [4] in you.” ⓕ

AMP.1: A Psalm of the sons of Korah. A Song. His foundation is on the holy mountain.
AMP.2:
The LORD loves the gates of Zion
More than all the dwellings of Jacob (Israel).
AMP.3:
Glorious things are spoken of you,
O city of God [Jerusalem]. Selah.
AMP.4:
"I will mention Rahab (Egypt) and Babylon among those who know Me—
Behold, Philistia and Tyre with Ethiopia (Cush)—
'This one was born there.'"
AMP.5:
But of Zion it will be said, "This one and that one were born in her,"
And the Most High Himself will establish her.
AMP.6:
The LORD will count, when He registers the peoples,
"This one was born there." Selah.
AMP.7:
The singers as well as the players of flutes will say,
"All my springs and sources of joy are in you [Jerusalem, city of God]."

MSG.1:
A Korah psalm
He founded Zion on the Holy Mountain—
MSG.2:
and oh, how God loves his home!
Loves it far better than all
the homes of Jacob put together!
MSG.3:
God's hometown—oh!
everyone there is talking about you!
MSG.4:
I name them off, those among whom I'm famous:
Egypt and Babylon,
also Philistia,
even Tyre, along with Cush.
Word's getting around; they point them out:
"This one was born again here!"
MSG.5:
The word's getting out on Zion:
"Men and women, right and left,
get born again in her!"
MSG.6:
God registers their names in his book:
"This one, this one, and this one—
born again, right here."
MSG.7:
Singers and dancers give credit to Zion:
"All my springs are in you!"

NIV.1:
Psalm 87
Of the Sons of Korah. A psalm. A song.
He has set his foundation on the holy mountain;
NIV.2:
the Lord loves the gates of Zion
more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
NIV.3:
Glorious things are said of you,
O city of God: Selah
NIV.4:
“I will record Rahab and Babylon
among those who acknowledge me—
Philistia too, and Tyre, along with Cush—
and will say, ‘This one was born in Zion.’”
NIV.5:
Indeed, of Zion it will be said,
“This one and that one were born in her,
and the Most High himself will establish her.”
NIV.6:
The Lord will write in the register of the peoples:
“This one was born in Zion.” Selah
NIV.7:
As they make music they will sing,
“All my fountains are in you.”

NRSVCE.1: On the holy mount stands the city he founded;
NRSVCE.2:
the LORD loves the gates of Zion
more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
NRSVCE.3:
Glorious things are spoken of you,
O city of God. Selah
NRSVCE.4:
Among those who know me I mention Rahab and Babylon;
Philistia too, and Tyre, with Ethiopia —
“This one was born there,” they say.
NRSVCE.5:
And of Zion it shall be said,
“This one and that one were born in it”;
for the Most High himself will establish it.
NRSVCE.6:
The LORD records, as he registers the peoples,
“This one was born there.” Selah
NRSVCE.7:
Singers and dancers alike say,
“All my springs are in you.”

NET.1: The LORD’s city is in the holy hills.
NET.2: The LORD loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwelling places of Jacob.
NET.3: People say wonderful things about you, O city of God. (Selah)
NET.4: I mention Rahab and Babylon to my followers. Here are Philistia and Tyre, along with Ethiopia. It is said of them, “This one was born there.”
NET.5: But it is said of Zion’s residents, “Each one of these was born in her, and the sovereign One makes her secure.”
NET.6: The LORD writes in the census book of the nations, “This one was born there.” (Selah)
NET.7: As for the singers, as well as the pipers — all of them sing within your walls.
